Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 060. Engravers[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

[a-na LUGAL EN-ia]

(1) [To the king, my lord: your servant Ṭ]ab-šar-Aššur. [Good health] to the king, my lord!

o 22

[ARAD-ka m]⸢DÙG⸣—IM—aš-⸢šur⸣

o 33

[lu DI-mu a]-⸢na⸣ LUGAL ⸢EN⸣-[ia]

o 44

[ina UGU LÚv].KAB.SAR-⸢MEŠ⸣

(4) As to the engravers [about whom the king my lord] wrote to me, [just as the kin]g my lord [commanded ......

o 55

[ša LUGAL be-lí] iš-pur-⸢an⸣-[ni]

o 66

[ki-i ša] ⸢LUGAL⸣ be-⸢lí⸣ [x x]

o 77

[x x x x x]+⸢x x⸣+[x x x]

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Reverse
rr uninscribed

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P336596/.
